data.title: Strategy buys $264M of bitcoin in past week, sells 1.57M shares of common stock
data.description:
Strategy (MSTR) acquired 2,932 bitcoins for $264.1M, the bitcoin treasury company disclosed on Monday.

To pay for the acquisition, the company sold ~1.57M shares of common stock for $257.0M in net proceeds and 70,201 shares of variable rate series A perpetual stretch preferred stock (STRC) for $7.0M in net proceeds.

Strategy (MSTR) stock slid 1.5% in early trading on Monday. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) rose 1.6% in the past 24 hours to $88.0K.

The company paid an average price of $90,061 per token for the bitcoin it acquired during Jan. 20-Jan. 25, 2026. 

The purchases bring Strategy's (MSTR) total bitcoin earnings to 712,647 for a purchase price of $54.2B at an average price of $76,037 per bitcoin. At the recent bitcoin price of $88.0K, Strategy's total holdings are valued at ~$62.7B.

data.title: Bank of Marin Bancorp Non-GAAP EPS of $0.59 beats by $0.07
data.description:
• Bank of Marin Bancorp press release (BMRC): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.59 beats by $0.07.

  • Total cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash were $225.3 million at December 31, 2025, compared to $219.3 million at September 30, 2025. The $6.0 million increase was mainly a result of the growth in deposits and the receipt of the $43.8 million in subordinated notes, net of issuance costs, partially offset by the increase in loans and investment purchases.

data.title: TotalEnergies gets extension of Libya’s Waha oil concession, signs deal to boost Libyan oil capacity
data.description:
TotalEnergies (TTE) said Monday it signed an agreement to extend the Waha oil concessions in Libya until the end of 2050, setting new financial terms to boost production from the fields.

The agreement sets new fiscal terms allowing for an increase in the production of the concessions that currently produce ~370K boe/day, which the company said will pave the way for a new phase of investments, including the development of the North Gialo field, which is expected to add 100K boe/d of production.

For Libya, the extension is significant as it underpins efforts to stabilize and increase oil production after years of political disruption and underinvestment.

The Waha concessions are held by Libya's National Oil Corp. with a 59.16% stake, alongside TotalEnergies (TTE) and ConocoPhillips (COP), which each hold 20.42%.

The Libyan government signed a deal with TotalEnergies (TTE) and ConocoPhillips (COP) that would more than double production capacity of their Waha venture, Prime Minister Dbeibah said Saturday.

The deal is aimed at boosting production capacity by up to 850K bbl/day from 340K-400K bbl/day under normal operations and is expected to generate net revenues of more than $376B, Dbeibah said.

Waha ‍operates five main oil and gas fields as well as several producing subfields, connected by pipeline networks that transport crude to the Sidra oil terminal and gas ⁠to processing facilities.

data.title: Ever-growing cloud migrations present pressure for smaller cybersecurity vendors: Morgan Stanley
data.description:
The continued migration of data workloads to the cloud will allow hyperscalers, such as Microsoft (MSFT), to grow their share of the cybersecurity market, according to Morgan Stanley.

Platform vendors, such as Palo Alto Networks (PANW) and CrowdStrike (CRWD), have the best chance to compete, while smaller vendors, such as Okta (OKTA) and Varonis Systems (VRNS), will undergo pressure.

This prompted the financial firm to downgrade Varonis to Equal-weight from Overweight.

"At ~14% of the overall cybersecurity software market (~11% with MSFT alone), with a combined business that has more than tripled in the last 5 years, the hyperscalers combined are the largest cyber vendors in the market today," said Morgan Stanley analysts, led by Meta Marshall, in a Monday investor report.

Morgan Stanley noted that 47% of workloads are now in the cloud versus 25% three years ago.

"With hyperscalers benefitting from the move of infrastructure further towards the cloud, AI being a catalyst for more workloads to move, and the hunt for more cost-effective solutions within cyber, we would expect the influence of these vendors to continue to grow," Marshall added. "We do not see the hyperscalers as being best-of-breed vendors by customers, but resellers do note that when CISO organizations report to the CFO, the 'included' value proposition is hard to forego."

The larger cybersecurity platforms, such as Palo Alto, CrowdStrike and Zscaler (ZS) are best positioned against the hyperscalers offering cybersecurity offerings, as they can also appeal to cost efficiency and rank as top names in the industry.

"A combination of more spend moving towards the cloud, cybersecurity remaining a top 3 IT spend market, and a $270bn TAM has made cybersecurity an increasing focus of at least some of the hyperscalers," Marshall said.

In addition to Microsoft, Google's (GOOG)(GOOGL) recent acquisition of Wiz, which is expected to close this year, will also allow it to make inroads in the cybersecurity realm. Google also acquired the cybersecurity firm Mandiant in 2022.

"Resellers of Wiz are seeing the pending acquisition impact sales more negatively, particularly if not large GCP customers, but we have heard from existing customers a strong desire to stay with Wiz post the acquisition," Marshall noted. "Amazon remains the quietest of the three in cybersecurity, largely just offering security products with AWS (AMZN) workloads, but nothing external facing."

data.title: American Axle to change name to Dauch Corporation, adopt new NYSE ticker
data.description:
• American Axle & Manufacturing Holdings (AXL) on Monday said that it has changed its name to Dauch Corporation, effective January 26, 2026, following an amendment to its certificate of incorporation filed in Delaware.
  • The company said its common stock will begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the new name and ticker symbol “DCH” effective February 5, 2026, replacing the current ticker “AXL.”
  • The CUSIP will remain unchanged, and existing stock certificates will continue to be valid.
  • The name change takes effect as the company prepares for its planned acquisition of Dowlais Group and its subsidiaries, which is expected to close on February 3, 2026.
  • The company said no action is required from stockholders in connection with the name or ticker symbol change.
  • AXL -0.12% premarket to $8.27.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Apple unveils long-awaited updated AirTag
data.description:
• Apple (AAPL) unveiled a new version of its popular AirTag item finder on Monday, which has a longer range than the previous model. 
  • The new AirTag, which is available for order on Apple's website today and will be available at Apple Store locations later this week, contains the tech giant's second-generation Ultra Wideband chip. This chip is found in the iPhone 17 lineup, iPhone Air, Apple Watch Ultra 3, and Apple Watch Series 11, and helps the new AirTag find items from up to 50% farther away than the previous model. It also sports an upgraded Bluetooth chip. The new AirTag also has a 50% louder chime than the previous generation. 
  • The second-generation AirTag is available in one- and four-packs for $29 and $99, respectively, Apple added. The first-generation AirTag was announced in 2021.
  • In addition, Apple also announced a new watch band for the Apple Watch on Monday.
  • Apple shares rose 1.4% in premarket trading.

data.title: West Point Gold announces equity offering of up to C$20 million
data.description:
• West Point Gold (WPG:CA) has announced an offer for sale of up to 18,181,900 common shares at an issue price of C$1.10 per share, for aggregate gross proceeds of up to C$20M.
  • The net proceeds will be used for exploration at the Gold Chain Project in Arizona, USA, and for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The closing date of the offering is scheduled to be on or about February 17, 2026
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Helium Evolution appoints Malcolm Adams as president, CEO and director
data.description:
• Helium Evolution (HEVI:CA) has announced the appointment of Malcolm Adams as President, Chief Executive Officer and Director, effective February 1, 2026.
  • Mr. Adams brings over 30 years of experience in energy, private equity and technology sectors. Source: Press Release

data.title: Bob's Discount Furniture aims for a $2.5B valuation in its IPO
data.description:
Home furnishings retailer Bob's Discount Furniture (BOBS) indicated on Monday that it aims to raise as much as $370 million in its IPO in the U.S.

The Manchester, Connecticut-based company plans to offer 19.45M shares in an expected range of $17 to $19 per share. At the top end of the range, Bob's (BOBS) would be valued at $2.5B. Following the IPO, Bain-advised investment funds and affiliates will still own roughly three-quarters of the outstanding common stock.

Bob’s Discount Furniture (BOBS) traces its roots to the early 1990s when founder Bob Kaufman opened the first store in Newington, Connecticut, opportunistically taking over a bankrupt furniture location during a recession and negotiating a low–fixed-cost lease tied to sales. The brand grew through the 1990s and 2000s across New England by emphasizing a no-frills store environment, everyday low pricing, and heavy use of quirky TV and radio ads featuring Kaufman himself as the on-air pitchman.

Over time, the company expanded beyond its regional base, built out a distribution network, and moved its headquarters to Manchester, Connecticut. The value furniture chain now has over 200 locations.

Looking ahead, Bob’s Discount Furniture (BOBS) is pursuing an aggressive physical-store growth strategy while also positioning itself for omnichannel competition. Industry insiders said the IPO filing signals an intent to fund further growth plans.

Bob’s Discount Furniture (BOBS) competes in a broad and fragmented landscape that includes national furniture chains, big-box and department stores, and pure-play e-commerce players. Haverty Furniture Companies (HVT), Ashley HomeStore, Macy's (M), Kohl's (KSS), Wayfair (W), Bassett Furniture (BSET), and Rooms To Go are all considered competitors to some degree.

data.title: Century Aluminum, EGA to build first U.S. aluminum smelter since 1980
data.description:
Century Aluminum (CENX) and Emirates Global Aluminum announced plans Monday to build the first new primary aluminum production plant in the U.S. since 1980, in a joint venture to be owned 60% by EGA and 40% by Century.

The new plant, to be built in Inola, Oklahoma, is expected to produce 750K metric tons/year of aluminum, larger than previously planned and more than doubling current U.S. production.

The companies said the project will use EGA's latest state-of-the-art EX technology, the most advanced ever installed in the U.S.

Construction of the project is expected to start by year-end 2026, with production planned to begin by the end of the decade; detailed engineering work is already underway, and negotiations for a long-term power supply are progressing.

"Our partner EGA brings world-class smelting technology and construction expertise that are fast-tracking our collective efforts to realize President Trump’s vision of rapidly increasing domestic primary aluminum production," Century Aluminum (CENX) CEO Jesse Gary said.
